approach: Isolated adversarial reconstruction of the compiled Erdős #530 interface, followed by a kernel-checked comparison with the repository's existing Sidon API and a minimal API-reuse refactor.

strategy.mechanism: Reconstruct the source meanings independently, detect repository API duplication, and compile a universal equivalence theorem before changing the candidate.

hypothesis: The candidate's finite-set Sidon predicate is extensionally equivalent for every Finset ℝ to the repository's reusable set-level IsSidon predicate, including repeated summands and swapped-pair triviality.
discriminating_test: Compile a separate Lean file that restates the removed finite predicate and proves an iff with project IsSidon for an arbitrary Finset ℝ, plus repeated-summand and swap controls.

search_efficiency.naive_space: The semantic domain contains all finite subsets of ℝ and is not finitely enumerable. A full repository build spans 975 local Lean source modules plus dependencies; the exact target previously reported an 8038-job dependency closure.
reductions_considered: universal theorem instead of larger finite enumeration
one named module target instead of all project roots
exact source hashes and byte comparison
search_efficiency.chosen_mechanism: A universally quantified Lean equivalence theorem eliminates the whole local-versus-project predicate ambiguity at once; the queued build requests only the named Problem 530 target.
search_efficiency.estimated_or_measured_savings: The semantic checker covered every Finset ℝ in 10.082 seconds rather than increasing the earlier 512-case cutoff. Build savings relative to a full lake build remain unmeasured; the 8038 dependency jobs were not eliminated.
search_efficiency.soundness_guard: The checker imports the project definition independently, restates the removed predicate literally, and was accepted by Lean 4.27.0 with empty stdout and stderr.

space_reduction.represented_space_after: One reusable Sidon definition and one refactored Problem 530 module awaiting its exact target build.
space_reduction.reduction_factor: Semantic ambiguity eliminated globally; build reduction factor unmeasured.
space_reduction.coverage_scope: Every Finset ℝ for predicate equivalence; exact Problem 530 module only for the queued build.
space_reduction.soundness_basis: Lean theorem legacyFinsetIsSidon_iff_projectIsSidon; exact source and driver hash prefilters.
space_reduction.remaining_unknown: The refactored source hash has not completed target elaboration, full CI, clean-checkout reproduction, or maintainer placement review.
space_reduction.next_bulk_elimination: Review the completed refactored-target lab once, then decide helper-definition placement before requesting any full build.

tactical_learning.prediction: The legacy finite predicate and repository predicate would agree, permitting API reuse without changing the mathematical statement.
tactical_learning.observation: Lean accepted the universal equivalence theorem and both edge controls with return code 0 and empty diagnostics.
tactical_learning.surprise: The mathematical semantics agreed, but the candidate unnecessarily duplicated an API already used by other Formal Conjectures files. The delegate's claimed elimination of 8037 build targets was also contradicted by the 8038-job target log.
tactical_learning.failure_signature: Importing the previously built target OLean locally exposed a stale mounted-cache path; a second isolated attempt used unavailable repository-specific attributes. Neither failure reached the equivalence theorem.
tactical_learning.bottleneck_update: The current bottleneck is kernel-checking source hash e27b8c80… and obtaining a maintainer-style decision on whether the extremal helper definitions belong in the problem file or FormalConjecturesForMathlib.

lab_review.reason: The durable record reports return code 0; source and driver hashes match the submitted inputs; stderr is empty; stdout records the warning-fatal target success and a nonempty 126000-byte OLean with the expected hash.

summary: The pre-refactor candidate's warning-fatal exact target build was validated. Independent review then found that the candidate duplicated the repository's existing IsSidon API. A separate Lean checker proved the old and project predicates equivalent for every finite real set and passed adversarial repeated-summand and swap controls. The candidate was refactored to reuse the project definition. Its new hash is queued/running in a checkpointed target build and is not yet claimed to compile.
rationale: The universal Lean equivalence is stronger and more relevant than another bounded enumeration and directly justifies the API refactor. The prior lab receipt supports only the old source hash, so the verification contract remains incomplete for the current candidate.
claims: For every Finset ℝ, the removed LegacyFinsetIsSidon predicate is logically equivalent to the repository's IsSidon predicate on its set coercion.
The pre-refactor candidate hash 208af82a29f35bf60d55f86669f7794cf30aae39d71eb24968f255b8cfa771c9 passed its exact warning-fatal Lean target build and produced a 126000-byte OLean.
The current candidate hash e27b8c80ed9edb57989006cc9bd299fdd58331c2b2a30b69ec9721b409562a2e reuses project IsSidon and has not yet been claimed to build.
The maintained problem page and issue #773 still present Problem #530 as open and without an accepted formalization.
evidence: Lean 4.27.0 accepted checks/Erdos530SemanticAudit.lean in experiment 20260721-223233-e53846, return code 0, empty stdout/stderr.
Validated lab-scout-bdf77f43574b-83c018dc57aa, return code 0, 886.529 seconds, exact target success.
Official source and issue inspected on 2026-07-21.
next_steps: Inspect durable completion state for lab-scout-bdf77f43574b-03d1a8ffc4cf without resubmitting it.
If the refactored target passes, conduct a maintainer-style placement review for GuaranteedSidonSize, ell, and supporting lemmas.
After placement is settled, run a clean-checkout target control and a checkpointed full lake --wfail build.
Seek human Formal Conjectures review and linked-PR acceptance; do not claim upstream formalization beforehand.
